text: When you write code, you will often need to find and fix errors, or "bugs." This process is called debugging. The first step is identifying the bug—you might notice your program crashes or gives the wrong output. A systematic approach helps you solve problems efficiently. Instead of guessing, check one part of your code at a time. Debugging tools, like breakpoints in an IDE, can pause the program and show you what is happening. Another useful technique is logging, where you add print statements to track variable values. By following these steps, you can find and fix bugs more easily and improve your code's reliability.
